name: How long does it usually take to install new flooring in an average Bay Area house?
headline: How long does it usually take to install new flooring in an average Bay Area house?

mainEntity.name: How long does it usually take to install new flooring in an average Bay Area house?
mainEntity.text: How long does it usually take to install new flooring in an average Bay Area house?

acceptedAnswer.text: The time it takes to install new flooring in an average house depends on the material, how much prep is needed, and how many rooms or obstacles there are. For a simple living room and hallway, laminate or vinyl plank might take 1–2 days once prep is done. A whole main floor of a typical Bay Area home can take 3–5 days. Site-finished hardwood usually takes longer because the wood must be installed, then sanded, stained, and coated. That can stretch to 1–2 weeks including drying time. Tile often falls somewhere in between, especially if the installer needs to build up the floor or do complex layouts. What often adds time is not the laying of the floor itself, but the prep: removing old flooring, fixing subfloor issues, leveling low spots, and dealing with doors, stairs, and trim. Moving furniture, appliances, and setting up dust protection also take time. To plan your schedule, ask your installer for a written timeline that includes demo, prep, installation, and any sanding or finishing. Also ask about cure times before you can move furniture back or walk on the floor with shoes. A common pitfall is booking painters, movers, or other trades too tightly around the flooring job, causing delays or damage.
